name: Eggplant Wonton Soup
recipeIngredient: 1 Red bell pepper, cut into quarters
1 lb Eggplant, sliced into 3/8-inch thick rounds
1/4 cup Mixed fresh herbs (such as parsley or cilantro)
recipeInstructions:
1. Char the red bell pepper on all sides and transfer to a covered bowl.
2. Grill the eggplant slices for at least 15 minutes.
3. Rub off the skins of both peppers and eggplant.
4. Seed, devein, and mince the jalapeno peppers.
5. Cut the eggplant into 1/4-inch dice.
6. Spray the olive oil on both sides of the eggplant and grill until golden brown.
7. Combine the eggplant, pepper, jalapeno, herbs, saffron, tofu, salt, and pepper in a bowl.
8. Place about half a tablespoon of filling in the center of each wonton wrapper.
9. Brush the edges of the wrapper with water and fold to form a semi-circle, sealing tightly.
10. Repeat with the remaining wrappers.
11. Lay parchment paper in a steam basket and arrange the wontons on top, sprayed lightly with olive oil between layers.
12. Steam over boiling water for 5 minutes, then set aside covered.
13. In a soup pot, reduce stock to 3 1/2 cups, add carrots and cook for 2-3 minutes.
14. Add snow peas and tomatoes, cooking for an additional minute.
15. Remove from heat, add the wontons, ginger, and cilantro.
16. Serve in shallow bowls, with each serving containing approximately 6 wontons, drizzled with olive oil.
17. Serve with Carrot Slaw and Pine Nut & Orange Wild Rice Salad for a complete lunch meal.
